title: Python implementation of the Arata et al. PCP simulations
description:
Python implementation of the Arata et al. planar cell polarity (PCP) simulation scripts.

This implementation preserves the Octave update rules and periodic-neighborhood logic. For reproducibility and consistency, explicit handling was added for differences between the published paper and the original scripts:

  • Arata_et_al_random divides mean angles by the number of cells, but Script 1 and the paper do not. The Python implementation defaults to the paper-consistent behavior. Use --legacy-script2-angle-scaling to reproduce Script 2 exactly.
  • The paper defines polarity magnitude as sqrt(Fx^2 + Fy^2) / sum(FM_i), but both Octave scripts omit the denominator. The Python implementation keeps the script behavior by default and offers --paper-magnitude as an option.
  • Arata_et_al_random allocates some temporary arrays with (Xmax, Ymax) instead of (Ymax, Xmax). Because the published runs use 24 x 24, the bug is masked there. The Python implementation fixes it.

Usage:

python random_distribution.py --outdir . --dirname Random_test_python
python patch_shift.py --outdir . --dirname patch_shift_python

python random_distribution.py --legacy-script2-angle-scaling
python random_distribution.py --paper-magnitude
python patch_shift.py --paper-magnitude
notes: Python reimplementation of the Arata et al. PCP simulations with documented handling of paper-versus-script discrepancies for reproducibility.
method: Software developed in Python to reproduce and document the planar cell polarity simulations described by Arata, Koyama, and Fujimori, while preserving Octave update rules and periodic-neighborhood logic and explicitly documenting implementation choices required for reproducibility.
